title: Hieracium szilyanum
taxonomy: Hieracium szilyanum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. This species belongs to the genus Hieracium, which comprises hawkweeds. The plant's classification is further divided into family (Asteraceae) and order (Asterales). Its classification also includes the kingdom Plantae and the rank of species. In a broader sense, it falls under the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida.

preparations: Hieracium szilyanum is a lesser-known species of hawkweed, and as such, information on its herbal preparations is limited. intro: Hieracium szilyanum, also known as the Hawkweed or Life Everlasting, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium szilyanum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Hieracium szilyanum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
